Q30.r: The scheme mandates a regeneration and replenishment obligation with an enforcement consequence: (1) The JFMC notification specifies 'Responsibilities: Forest protection, regeneration, conflict resolution' as mandatory committee duties. (2) The scheme operates on a '3-year renewable MOU (performance-based)' — non-renewal (loss of management rights, gate fee income, employment continuation) is the defined consequence for failing to meet regeneration performance obligations. A performance-based MOU that conditions continued access to scheme benefits on regeneration output meets the T4.1 criterion of 'non-compliance has a defined consequence.' (3) CAG confirms 92% JFMC renewal rate — the 8% non-renewal indicates the consequence mechanism is actively applied. (4) Outcome Budget confirms ongoing 3-year monitoring of vegetation density as the performance metric that governs MOU renewal.
Q30.src: Official Notification No. 6-JFM/1999/CGN (2023) — regeneration as mandatory JFMC responsibility; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 ('3-year renewable MOU performance-based'); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 ('92% JFMC renewal rate — MOU analysis'); Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q31.r: Carried T1.1 = A1 (state forest land — natural/physical commons). Per the track selection rule: T1.1 ≠ E → track = RhoRatio. Q32 is answered; Q33 is set to SKIP.

Q32.calc: ρ = Annual extraction ÷ Annual replenishment. Replenishment (measured): Biomass accumulation = 45 tons/hectare over 3-year monitoring period = 15 t/ha/year net (CAG-verified, Outcome Budget confirmed). Extraction (estimated): NTFP/herb collection per standard JFM operational norms for regenerating degraded forest ≈ 1.5–2.0 t/ha/year (not directly reported; estimated from NTFP extraction norms for similar JFM operations in central Indian tribal forest belt). Upper-bound estimate: ρ = 2.0 ÷ 15 = 0.13. Lower bound: ρ = 1.5 ÷ 15 = 0.10.
Q32.interp: PASS — ρ ≤ 0.13 (upper bound) is well below the 1.2 threshold. Biomass increased from 12 t/ha baseline to 57 t/ha (375% increase), confirming net replenishment far exceeds extraction. Even under conservative extraction assumptions, ρ << 1.0. Note: T1.5 = Yes (boundaries demarcated) — replenishment component is Measured via CAG-verified biomass inventory; extraction component is Estimated from NTFP operational norms as the source documents do not separately report annual extraction volumes. Overall ρ is classified Estimated due to the estimated extraction component.
Q32.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 ('Biomass: 45 tons/hectare measured — inventory plots'); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 ('Biomass accumulation: 45 tons/hectare added from baseline 12 tons/hectare'); Outcome Budget 2025-26; NTFP extraction norms: Ministry of Environment Forest and Climate Change JFM operational guidelines (central Indian tribal forest belt reference)
docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — biomass 45 tons/hectare measured via inventory plots
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — biomass accumulation and 3-year monitoring data
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — vegetation improvement 62% area, biomass 45 tons/hectare confirmed
